assumptions_fr: La question vise les comptes consolidés du groupe, et non les comptes individuels ou séparés.

La créance de dividende intragroupe a déjà été comptabilisée et constitue donc un poste monétaire intragroupe libellé dans une devise pouvant être différente de la monnaie fonctionnelle d'au moins une des entités concernées.
Aucune désignation n'est analysée ici au titre d'une couverture d'investissement net dans une activité à l'étranger ; la question porte sur le risque de change d'une créance de dividende intragroupe.

recommendation.justification: En consolidation, une créance intragroupe n'est en principe pas éligible, sauf pour le risque de change d'un poste monétaire intragroupe non totalement éliminé en consolidation (IFRS 9 6.3.5–6.3.6). Donc une documentation de couverture n'est possible que si la créance de dividende crée bien une exposition de change résiduelle au niveau consolidé.

approaches.reasoning_fr: La créance de dividende déjà comptabilisée entre dans la catégorie d'un actif reconnu au sens d'IFRS 9 6.3.1. Mais, en comptes consolidés, un élément intragroupe n'est normalement pas éligible ; l'exception d'IFRS 9 6.3.6 permet toutefois de désigner le seul risque de change d'un poste monétaire intragroupe s'il génère des écarts de change non totalement éliminés en consolidation. Donc, dans votre situation, la couverture n'est envisageable qu'à cette condition précise (IFRS 9 6.3.1, 6.3.5, 6.3.6).
conditions_fr: La créance de dividende doit être un poste monétaire intragroupe exposé au change.
Les écarts de change sur cette créance ne doivent pas être totalement éliminés en consolidation.
La désignation doit porter sur le risque de change, et non sur l'élément intragroupe en tant que tel.
approaches.practical_implication_fr: Possible en consolidation seulement pour la composante change résiduelle du poste monétaire intragroupe.

references.excerpt: A hedged item can be a recognised asset or liability, an unrecognised firm commitment, a forecast transaction or a net investment in a foreign operation. The hedged item can be: (a)a single item; or (b)a group of items (subject to paragraphs 6.6.1⁠–⁠6.6.6 and B6.6.1⁠–⁠B6.6.16). **A hedged item can also be a component of such an item or group of items** (see paragraphs 6.3.7 and B6.3.7⁠–⁠B6.3.25).

For hedge accounting purposes, **only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with a party external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items**. Hedge accounting can be applied to transactions between entities in the same group only in the individual or separate financial statements of those entities and not in the consolidated financial statements of the group, except for the consolidated financial statements of an investment entity, as defined in IFRS 10, where transactions between an investment entity and its subsidiaries measured at fair value through profit or loss will not be eliminated in the consolidated financial statements.

However, as an exception to paragraph 6.3.5, **the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item** (for example, a payable/receivable between two subsidiaries) **may qualify as a hedged item in the consolidated financial statements if it results in an exposure to foreign exchange rate gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation** in accordance with IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates. In accordance with IAS 21, foreign exchange rate gains and losses on intragroup monetary items are not fully eliminated on consolidation when the intragroup monetary item is transacted between two group entities that have different functional currencies. In addition, the foreign currency risk of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements provided that the transaction is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into that transaction and the foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.reasoning_fr: Cette approche vise notamment les transactions prévues hautement probables ; or, ici, le dividende intragroupe a déjà donné lieu à la comptabilisation d'une créance, donc il ne s'agit plus d'une transaction future au sens d'IFRS 9 6.3.1 et 6.3.6. En outre, IFRS 9 B6.3.5 réserve l'exception intragroupe aux cas où le risque de change affectera le résultat consolidé, ce qui n'est pas présenté ici pour un dividende intragroupe déjà constaté (IFRS 9 6.3.1, 6.3.6, B6.3.5, B6.3.6).
approaches.practical_implication_fr: La couverture de flux de trésorerie n'est pas le bon modèle pour une créance de dividende intragroupe déjà reconnue.

references.excerpt: A hedged item can be a recognised asset or liability, an unrecognised firm commitment, **a forecast transaction** or a net investment in a foreign operation. The hedged item can be: (a)a single item; or (b)a group of items (subject to paragraphs 6.6.1⁠–⁠6.6.6 and B6.6.1⁠–⁠B6.6.16). A hedged item can also be a component of such an item or group of items (see paragraphs 6.3.7 and B6.3.7⁠–⁠B6.3.25).

However, as an exception to paragraph 6.3.5, the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item (for example, a payable/receivable between two subsidiaries) may qualify as a hedged item in the consolidated financial statements if it results in an exposure to foreign exchange rate gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation in accordance with IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates. In accordance with IAS 21, foreign exchange rate gains and losses on intragroup monetary items are not fully eliminated on consolidation when the intragroup monetary item is transacted between two group entities that have different functional currencies. **In addition, the foreign currency risk of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements** provided that the transaction is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into that transaction and the foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.

Paragraph 6.3.6 states that in consolidated financial statements **the foreign currency risk of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction may qualify as a hedged item in a cash flow hedge**, provided that the transaction is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into that transaction and that the foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss. For this purpose an entity can be a parent, subsidiary, associate, joint arrangement or branch. **If the foreign currency risk of a forecast intragroup transaction does not affect consolidated profit or loss, the intragroup transaction cannot qualify as a hedged item.** This is usually the case for royalty payments, interest payments or management charges between members of the same group, unless there is a related external transaction. However, when the foreign currency risk of a forecast intragroup transaction will affect consolidated profit or loss, the intragroup transaction can qualify as a hedged item.

If a hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction qualifies for hedge accounting, **any gain or loss is recognised in, and taken out of, other comprehensive income in accordance with paragraph 6.5.11. The relevant period or periods during which the foreign currency risk of the hedged transaction affects profit or loss is when it affects consolidated profit or loss.**

approaches.label_fr: Couverture d'un investissement net dans une activité à l'étranger
approaches.reasoning_fr: Le modèle de couverture d'investissement net concerne l'exposition entre la monnaie fonctionnelle de l'activité étrangère et celle d'une société mère, et non une créance de dividende intragroupe déjà comptabilisée (IFRIC 16 10, 12). Votre question vise un dividende intragroupe à recevoir ; ce n'est pas, d'après les textes fournis, la couverture d'un investissement net dans une activité à l'étranger (IFRS 9 6.3.1 ; IFRIC 16 10, 12).
approaches.practical_implication_fr: Ce modèle n'est pas adapté à la couverture du change sur une créance de dividende intragroupe.

references.excerpt: A hedged item can be a recognised asset or liability, an unrecognised firm commitment, a forecast transaction or **a net investment in a foreign operation**. The hedged item can be: (a)a single item; or (b)a group of items (subject to paragraphs 6.6.1⁠–⁠6.6.6 and B6.6.1⁠–⁠B6.6.16). A hedged item can also be a component of such an item or group of items (see paragraphs 6.3.7 and B6.3.7⁠–⁠B6.3.25).
Hedge accounting may be applied only to **the foreign exchange differences arising between the functional currency of the foreign operation and the parent entity’s functional currency**.

The hedged risk may be designated as **the foreign currency exposure arising between the functional currency of the foreign operation and the functional currency of any parent entity** (the immediate, intermediate or ultimate parent entity) of that foreign operation. The fact that the net investment is held through an intermediate parent does not affect the nature of the economic risk arising from the foreign currency exposure to the ultimate parent entity.
